name: Eresma River Trail
name_original: Sendero del río Eresma (PRC-VA 42) — circular desde Hornillos de Eresma
description: Hike the Sendero del río Eresma (PRC-VA 42), a 5.1-mile circular route from Hornillos de Eresma, featuring river views and pine forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Hornillos De Eresma offers a network of trails primarily characterized by flat, open countryside and riverine landscapes. The region features gentle terrain, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for easy hikes around Hornillos De Eresma. Trails often follow the Eresma River, providing scenic paths through agricultural areas and natural riverbanks. This area is ideal for those seeking accessible routes and circular walks Hornillos De Eresma. Best hiking trails around Hornillos De Eresma The most popular hiking route is Eresma River Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the tranquil banks of the Eresma River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino de Nivaria Trail, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.4 km) path. This trail traverses open landscapes, offering expansive views of the surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Olmedo City Walls Trail, a 1.7 miles (2.8 km) trail leading through historical areas and around the town, often completed in about 44 minutes. Hiking around Hornillos De Eresma is defined by riverbank paths, open agricultural fields, and gentle, rolling terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, including beginner hiking routes Hornillos De Eresma. The routes in Hornillos De Eresma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 4 reviews. More than 15 hikers have used komoot to explore Hornillos De Eresma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: The hiking trails around Hornillos De Eresma are primarily characterized by &lt;em&gt;flat, open countryside&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;riverine landscapes&lt;/em&gt;. You'll find gentle terrain with minimal elevation changes, often following the Eresma River through agricultural areas and natural riverbanks.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for beginners. The terrain is generally flat with minimal elevation, making it ideal for those new to hiking. An excellent easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084863275/olmedo-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.7 miles (2.8 km) long and takes about 44 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Yes, Hornillos De Eresma offers several circular routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103650451/path-from-llano-to-valhondo-sl-va-56-circular-from-mojados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path from Llano to Valhondo (SL-VA 56) — circular from Mojados&lt;/a&gt;, or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079271497/on-the-banks-of-the-eresma-river-circular-from-alcazaren" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Eresma River — circular from Alcazarén&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: The routes in Hornillos De Eresma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil river paths and the expansive views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Hornillos De Eresma suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many of the trails are suitable for families. The generally flat and gentle terrain, especially along the Eresma River, makes for pleasant walks that are manageable for children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079262456/eresma-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eresma River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that follows tranquil banks and is popular with families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Most trails in the open countryside and along riverbanks are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. The open landscapes of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079259238/camino-de-nivaria-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Nivaria Trail&lt;/a&gt; are often enjoyed by dog walkers.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking routes?
A: While hiking, you can explore several historical and cultural landmarks. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3505363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Olmedo&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5783370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City Walls&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5780020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Church in Mojados&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3531358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Boal de Matapozuelos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Hornillos De Eresma. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this season. Winter offers a different charm, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: While the immediate trails might be rural, towns like Olmedo and Mojados, which are often starting or ending points for some routes, offer local cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your hike. It's a good idea to check local opening hours, especially in smaller villages.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084863275/olmedo-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079262456/eresma-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eresma River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours. Longer circular routes can extend to 3-4 hours.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Given the riverine and agricultural landscapes, you might spot various bird species, including waterfowl along the Eresma River. Small mammals and insects are also common. Keep an eye out for local flora that thrives in these open, rural environments.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas around Hornillos De Eresma?
A: Public transport options to Hornillos De Eresma itself might be limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the trailheads, especially for routes that start in smaller villages or more rural areas. Some larger nearby towns like Olmedo or Mojados may have better bus connections, from which you could potentially start a hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sendero del río Eresma (PRC-VA 42)?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Hornillos de Eresma. You can typically find parking within the village itself, close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered very easy and suitable for all ages, including beginners and families. It is predominantly flat with minimal elevation gain, making it a comfortable and pleasant walk.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You will walk on mostly flat terrain, winding along the lush riparian forest of the Eresma River. The trail also passes through pine forests, featuring Scots pine and maritime pine, and includes sections that run parallel to the river. You'll experience a unique natural setting within the generally arid Castilian steppe.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or natural features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you will encounter the historic Mediana Bridge (also known as Puente Viejo). The trail also features a fluvial beach called Valdigüete near the bridge, and you'll pass through an area of wineries on the eastern edge of Hornillos de Eresma.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete the 8.2 km route is approximately 2 hours and 6 minutes, though this can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero del río Eresma (PRC-VA 42) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The route is recommended for hiking throughout the entire year. Its diverse scenery offers different charms in every season, from lush greenery in spring and summer to unique colors in autumn and winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Sendero del río Eresma (PRC-VA 42). It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is there a good spot to cool off during warmer months?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the Mediana Bridge, you'll find the Valdigüete fluvial beach. This is a great spot to refresh yourself in the Eresma River during the summer.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the Sendero del Río Eresma, the route also briefly follows sections of the Cañada Real de Cáceres, an ancient livestock trail, and passes through local paths like Calle Espacio Concejil 1 and Calle del Arroyo.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Hornillos de Eresma?
faqs.answer: Hornillos de Eresma is a small village, but you may find local establishments offering refreshments or rural accommodation. It's advisable to check in advance for current availability of cafes, pubs, or restaurants.
